source.title: Cortical substrates for exploratory decisions in humans

confidence_rationale: Foundational fMRI dissociation: frontopolar cortex (and intraparietal sulcus) preferentially active during exploratory choices in a four-armed bandit; striatum and vmPFC active during exploitative choices. Establishes the neural substrate distinction that Q-043 presupposes — a directed/uncertainty-driven system separate from a value/exploit system. Source quality high (Nature, large-cohort fMRI with computational model fit); mapping fidelity moderate because the paper does not directly contrast a state-independent noise mechanism (MECH-313) against a state-dependent uncertainty-bonus mechanism (MECH-314) — it dissociates exploration from exploitation, leaving the within-exploration sub-decomposition to later work.
failure_signatures: Daw 2006's computational model used a softmax (exp-of-value) policy with no explicit uncertainty bonus on choice — instead, exploration was driven by the temperature parameter and Kalman-filter variance feeding indirectly into value updates. This means the paper alone cannot adjudicate whether the frontopolar-cortex effect they observed was driven by a state-independent noise floor (MECH-313 analog) or by a state-dependent uncertainty bonus (MECH-314 analog). Later work (Zajkowski 2017, Warren 2017, Gershman 2018) was needed to make that within-exploration distinction.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Human exploratory and exploitative decisions are dissociable neurally: frontopolar cortex and intraparietal sulcus are preferentially active during exploration, striatum and vmPFC during exploitation, in a gambling task formalised by a softmax-RL model.
mapping.ree_translation: REE substrate has an exploration locus (MECH-314 structured curiosity ≈ frontopolar uncertainty-driven bonus, per ARC-065 family doc) and an exploitation locus (E3 score path through striatum-analog and vmPFC-analog). Daw 2006 establishes the foundational neural-substrate dissociation Q-043 presupposes — the question of relative weighting only makes sense if the two mechanisms are independently real, and Daw is the canonical evidence that they are.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Daw 2006 dissociates exploration from exploitation, not state-independent noise from state-dependent uncertainty bonus. The within-exploration decomposition that Q-043 actually asks about (relative weights of MECH-313 vs MECH-314) is not directly addressed; this paper is anchor evidence for the existence of the directed/uncertainty system, not for its calibration vs noise. Treat as load-bearing for the EXISTENCE premise of Q-043; non-load-bearing for the CALIBRATION verdict.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI study (n=14), four-armed bandit gambling task with drifting reward distributions, computational reinforcement-learning model fit per-subject. Healthy young adults.

confidence_components.notes: Nature publication with large impact and replicated structure; small sample by modern standards but methodologically clean. Mapping fidelity is moderate because the paper's exploration/exploitation dichotomy does not subdivide into the state-independent vs state-dependent axis Q-043 needs. Transfer risk low: human RL task; REE substrate is also human-like RL agent at the relevant level of abstraction.
